name: agent-browser-clawdbot
tagline: ClawHub (446★): Headless browser automation CLI optimized for AI agents with accessibility tree snapshots and ref-based element selection
verdict: Solid trust profile: security A, quality B. But the multi-step workflow -- network navigation, an external CLI, file-based state -- has zero guidance on failure: a broken command or missing file leaves you guessing. No version history, and no clear trigger phrasing in the description either.

highlights.summary: The whole multi-step workflow (network navigation, external CLI tool, file-based state load/save, installation) has zero mention anywhere of what to do if a command fails, the tool
No version field in frontmatter, no changelog section, and no dated revision notes anywhere in the file.
Global install with no version pin (`@latest` implied); publisher is credited (Vercel Labs) so not a typosquat/rogue-registry case, but unpinned install of a well-known package is
